questions.question_text: How many total hours of pre-license education does Georgia require for a salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Georgia requires 75 instructional hours of pre-license education — a single GREC-approved Salesperson Prelicense Course (Rule 520-2-.04), not separate named courses. Alternatives: 10 quarter hours or 6 semester hours of eligible college coursework, or 75 prelicense hours approved by another state's real estate regulator.

questions.question_text: An applicant wants to qualify for the Georgia salesperson exam through education at a GREC-approved school. What must they complete?
distractors: Three 25-hour courses: Principles, Practice, and Legal Issues
A 60-hour course plus a 15-hour Georgia supplement
A 25-hour course followed by a 50-hour internship
questions.explanation: GREC Rule 520-2-.04 defines one integrated 75-hour Salesperson Prelicense Course covering 16 required content areas, including Georgia license law. Georgia does not break prelicense education into separately named courses. College coursework (10 quarter/6 semester hours) or another state's approved 75-hour course can substitute.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed 75 hours at an online school that is not approved by GREC or by any other state's real estate regulator. Can they sit for the Georgia salesperson exam?
questions.correct_answer: No, the coursework does not satisfy any qualifying route
distractors: Yes, any 75 hours of real estate coursework qualifies
Yes, if they pass a preliminary assessment
Only if they complete extra supervised hours
questions.explanation: Georgia accepts three education routes: the 75-hour prelicense course from a GREC-approved school, 10 quarter hours or 6 semester hours of eligible college coursework, or 75 prelicense hours approved by another state's or Canadian province's real estate regulator. A course from a school holding none of these approvals does not qualify.

questions.question_text: Georgia's required pre-license education for a salesperson consists of what?
distractors: Three 25-hour courses: Principles, Practice, and Legal Issues
A 45-hour national course and a 30-hour Georgia course
A 75-hour course plus a mandatory 25-hour exam prep course
questions.explanation: O.C.G.A. Section 43-40-8 requires at least 75 instructional hours in a salesperson's course of study approved by the Commission, and Rule 520-2-.04 defines it as a single course spanning 16 content areas from contracts and agency to Georgia license law.

questions.question_text: A student partially completed a GREC-approved school's salesperson prelicense program, which is offered as a series of shorter courses, and wants to finish at a different school. When may the second school credit the completed courses?
questions.correct_answer: Only if the new school offers the identical series of courses
distractors: Never — all 75 hours must be completed at one school
Only after GREC issues a written transfer waiver
questions.explanation: Under Rule 520-2-.02, credit for courses completed in a series that adds up to the 75-hour requirement may not be transferred to another school unless the new school offers the identical series. Being GREC-approved alone does not make partial credit transferable.

questions.question_text: A new Georgia salesperson obtained their license in February. By when must they complete post-license education?
questions.correct_answer: Within the first year of licensure
questions.explanation: Georgia requires completion of the 25-hour post-license course within the first year of licensure. This course reinforces practical skills for new licensees transitioning into active practice.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education must a Georgia licensee complete each four-year renewal cycle?
questions.explanation: Georgia requires 36 hours of CE every four years. Within those 36 hours, at least 3 hours must cover license law. Additionally, 9 hours from the post-license course count toward the first CE cycle.

questions.question_text: A licensee's birthday is in October and their license expires in October 2026. What is the earliest they can renew?
questions.explanation: Georgia allows early renewal up to 120 days before the expiration date. Licenses renew on the licensee's birthday month every four years, and there is no grace period after expiration.

questions.question_text: A licensee forgets to renew by their birthday month. How much grace period does Georgia allow?
distractors: 30-day grace period with a late fee
questions.explanation: Georgia provides no grace period for license renewal. Once the birthday month passes without renewal, the license expires and the licensee cannot legally practice until properly reinstated.

questions.question_text: Of the 36 hours of CE required each cycle, how many must specifically cover Georgia license law?
questions.explanation: Georgia mandates at least 3 hours of license law within each 36-hour CE cycle. This ensures licensees stay current with regulatory changes and understand their legal obligations under Georgia law.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a Georgia real estate license?
questions.explanation: Georgia requires applicants to be at least 18 years old. This aligns with the legal age to enter binding contracts, which is essential for real estate transactions.

questions.question_text: A veteran fails the Georgia salesperson exam and wants veteran's preference points applied. What must they mail to PSI within 60 days?
questions.correct_answer: Their original score report and a copy of their DD-214
distractors: Certificate of completion from MOS training
questions.explanation: Honorably discharged veterans with at least one year of active duty including at least 90 days during wartime may claim preference points, but only after failing to achieve a passing score. The candidate mails the original score report and a copy of DD Form 214 to PSI within 60 days (Rule 520-1-.04(3)).

questions.question_text: Which testing vendor administers the Georgia real estate licensing exam?
questions.explanation: Georgia contracts with PSI to administer the real estate licensing examination. Candidates must register and schedule through PSI after completing their pre-license education requirements.

questions.question_text: A license applicant who is a permanent resident but not a US citizen asks if they can apply. What should they be told?
questions.correct_answer: Legal residents are eligible to apply
distractors: They need a special GREC waiver
They must wait five years of residency
questions.explanation: Georgia allows both US citizens and legal residents to apply for a real estate license. The requirement is lawful presence, not citizenship, so permanent residents and other legal residents qualify.

questions.question_text: As part of the Georgia licensing process, applicants must complete which security step?
questions.correct_answer: A certified criminal history (GCIC) background report
distractors: Fingerprint submission through the state's fingerprinting vendor
questions.explanation: Each applicant must obtain a certified Georgia Crime Information Center (GCIC) criminal history report, issued no more than 60 days before applying, available at most sheriff's offices or police stations (non-residents submit an equivalent report from their state). Georgia does not require fingerprinting for a real estate license; any conviction on the report triggers a background investigation.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed Georgia salesperson wants to practice independently without a broker. Is this permitted?
questions.correct_answer: No, salespersons must work under a broker
questions.explanation: Georgia salespersons must operate under a sponsoring broker at all times. Independent practice requires a broker license, which has additional education and experience requirements beyond the salesperson level.

questions.question_text: What distinguishes an associate broker from a salesperson in Georgia?
questions.correct_answer: An associate broker holds a broker license under another broker
distractors: An associate broker may operate an independent brokerage in their own name
An associate broker needs fewer education hours
An associate broker works without any broker affiliation
questions.explanation: An associate broker has met all broker qualifications and holds a broker-level license but practices under another broker's firm rather than operating independently. A salesperson holds only the salesperson license. Operating one's own firm requires licensure as the firm's broker/qualifying broker.

questions.question_text: A salesperson's sponsoring broker retires and closes the firm. What happens to the salesperson's license?
distractors: The license transfers to another broker
The license remains active for 90 days
questions.explanation: When a sponsoring broker closes, the salesperson's license becomes inactive because it must be held under a sponsoring broker. The salesperson must find a new broker to reactivate their license.

questions.question_text: What pre-license education is required specifically for a Community Association Manager (CAM) license in Georgia?
questions.explanation: Georgia's CAM license requires 25 hours of pre-license education on a separate track from salesperson licensing. This focused curriculum covers community association management rather than traditional real estate practice.

questions.question_text: Which Georgia license type allows independent practice and supervision of other licensees?
questions.explanation: Only a broker license permits independent practice and the authority to supervise salespersons and associate brokers. Associate brokers hold broker-level qualifications but choose to work under another broker.
